metadata.description: Primary medial knee stabilizer resisting valgus stress.

metadata.description: C-shaped medial meniscus distributing load and resisting shear at the medial compartment.

metadata.description: More mobile lateral meniscus with popliteus hiatus; commonly torn with ACL injuries.

metadata.description: Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.

metadata.description: Anterior cuff tendon providing internal rotation and anterior stability.

metadata.description: Superior labrum and biceps anchor complex at the glenoid origin.

Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Osteochondral knee defects are focal cartilage-bone lesions where lesion stability, location, and symptoms drive operative strategy.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee pathoanatomy centers on articular-cartilage, femoral-condyles, tibial-plateau and related stabilizers.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating osteochondral defect of the knee.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for osteochondral defect of the knee — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for osteochondral defect of the knee.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for osteochondral defect of the knee require attending-defined progression gates.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Lesion stability on imaging and exam separates observation from operative fixation or grafting.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from osteochondral defect of the knee despite appropriate initial management
metadata.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
metadata.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after osteochondral defect of the knee treatment
metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Defect of the Knee Neighborhood
